title: Governed financial operating slice plan defined
objective: Define the smallest end-to-end financial workflows that deliver self-sustaining value through read-only aggregation first, bounded recommendation second, explicit human approval third, reversible execution fourth, and proof fifth. Every workflow is bound to a single canonical data authority, runs simulation-first inside a reversible policy-cell, carries mandatory Needs-Human-Approval before any state-changing rung, prohibits autonomous spend / trading / account creation / credential access / external financial action, keeps a witnessed audit trail, has a rollback that touches no real financial account, and proves its self-sustaining value. Observation precedes action in every workflow.
design_artifact_role: This file is the governed financial slice contract named in MVP1-001.target_artifacts. It encodes every financial state class exactly once with owner, lifetime, sync edge, and recovery per STORE-001, and it enumerates the concrete workflow set with the eight verification-gate dimensions plus the observation-precedes-action ladder.
source_grounding: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Blueprint - LifeOS Core Foundation.md (tiered memory: PostgreSQL+RuVector canonical, redb transient, envctl bridge, nu_plugin ingestion, ATAS/ESN resource-burn simulation, SHAKE256 witness chains, COW branching)
1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv (canonical vs temporary/derived state ownership per component)
generated/store_relationship_decision.json (STORE-001 keystone: single durable writer = PostgreSQL+RuVector; redb transient; forward-only sync; verified cutover)

1.0_VISION/LifeOS Master Plan - Consolidated v1 (2026-07-07).md (§1 Constitution: no unauthorized spend / no legal bypass; §1.5 MVP1 domain = financial+legal self-sustained growth, Element Ark Holdings; §2 cell types + Execution gates §11 Needs-Human-Approval for spend/trading/account creation/credentials/external action; §18 proof spine)
1.0_VISION/NORTH_STAR.md (NS-CLARITY see the whole; NS-DIRECTION direct as one system; NS-PROGRESS advance every domain)
generated/task_graph.source.csv rows MVP1-001, LIFEOS-006 (parent), LIFEOS-003 (execution spine), LIFEOS-005 (memory/compute substrate)

stages: identity/authority (David is final human authority; Finance Governance Agent may propose/aggregate, never execute raw financial action)
policy (Constitution §6 + Execution gates §11: spend/trading/account/credential/external = automatic Needs-Human-Approval)
memory (canonical financial state on PostgreSQL+RuVector; transient reads on redb per STORE-001)
simulation (sim-cell dry-run under COW branch; ESN resource-burn forecast for runway) before any render or action
execution (only rungs 1-2 authorized by this planning task; rungs 3-5 defined but gated behind human approval and out of scope here)
proof (per-workflow value_proof recorded to the proof ledger; no completion without proof)
rollback (restore archived financial plan; no financial account/transaction/credential touched)
next-action (decompose action-capable financial tasks only after this read-only slice is proven)

maturity_ladder.doctrine: observation precedes action; a workflow may not advance a rung until the prior rung is proven

rungs.name: read-only aggregation
rungs.gate: no write escapes the sim-cell; canonical reads only

rungs.name: bounded recommendation
rungs.gate: recommendation is advisory constraint only; prefixed 'LifeOS suggests:'; no external effect

rungs.name: explicit human approval
rungs.gate: mandatory Needs-Human-Approval from David before any state-changing action

rungs.name: reversible execution
rungs.gate: policy-cell with snapshot; COW branch; discardable; no irreversible action without explicit authority

rungs.name: proof
rungs.gate: witnessed proof record of the executed action and its reversibility point

maturity_ladder.mvp1_001_scope: This planning task authorizes only rungs 1-2 (read-only aggregation + bounded recommendation). No spend, trading, account creation, credential access, or external financial action is defined as executable here; rungs 3-5 are specified so downstream action-capable tasks inherit the gates, but product_execution_performed is false.

financial_state_authority.note: Every financial state class is assigned exactly once, specializing the STORE-001 keystone to the financial domain. PostgreSQL+RuVector is the single durable writer; redb is the transient tier; envctl is the forward-only bridge; the financial ingestion path reuses the nu_plugin CodeDB pattern (parse -> redb buffer -> envctl embed/commit -> PostgreSQL).

classes.sync_edge: external statement/feed -> nu_plugin parse -> redb buffer -> envctl commit -> PostgreSQL
classes.tier_relationship: canonical; redb only buffers the in-flight refresh

classes.sync_edge: nu_plugin parse -> redb buffer -> envctl commit -> PostgreSQL
nu_plugin parse -> redb buffer -> envctl commit -> PostgreSQL
local model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) during envctl bridge -> PostgreSQL vectors
classes.tier_relationship: canonical; redb performs f32 distance math only as a cache, no SQL/index/vector logic

classes.sync_edge: computed in sim-cell over PostgreSQL rows -> committed back to PostgreSQL

classes.lifetime: durable macro-state (positions only; NO trading capability)
classes.sync_edge: nu_plugin parse -> redb buffer -> envctl commit -> PostgreSQL
classes.tier_relationship: canonical; live quotes are transient on redb

classes.sync_edge: redb -> envctl flush -> PostgreSQL (only settled significant values persist)
classes.recovery: redb local disk; transient by definition
classes.tier_relationship: intended terminal transient role; never a second durable writer

classes.sync_edge: none; prevents re-ingesting/re-embedding an identical statement block
classes.tier_relationship: redb intended dedup-cache role (blueprint semantic cache)

classes.sync_edge: only the settled human-approval decision persists forward to PostgreSQL; the draft itself does not become durable truth
classes.recovery: regenerable; provenance links back to PostgreSQL source rows
classes.tier_relationship: non-authoritative for shared macro-state; witnessed by SHAKE256 chains rooted in PostgreSQL

classes.sync_edge: every read, recommendation, and (future) approval is bound to a source vector in PostgreSQL
classes.tier_relationship: canonical; anti-bluffing root; a recommendation with no causal edge to real ledger rows is rejected

classes.sync_edge: only the proven forecast summary persists to PostgreSQL; failed worlds become failure capsules, never baseline
classes.tier_relationship: non-authoritative forecast; blueprint ATAS resource-burn maps to financial runway

classes.lifetime: durable secret, out of database scope
classes.sync_edge: n/a; PostgreSQL stores references/provenance, never raw secrets; redb never holds secrets
classes.tier_relationship: credential access is a prohibited path for this slice

financial_state_authority.assignment_invariant: 13 financial state classes, each assigned to exactly one canonical owner; no class has two durable writers; redb never becomes a second durable authority for a PostgreSQL-owned class (STORE-001 single-writer invariant).

global_prohibitions.policy_basis: Master Plan Constitution §6 (no unauthorized spend, no legal bypass, no real-world execution without authority) + Execution gates §11 (automatic Needs-Human-Approval for spend, trading, account creation, credential handling, external action, anything irreversible)
global_prohibitions.enforcement: Every workflow is read-only at rungs 1-2; any transition to a state-changing rung raises a mandatory Needs-Human-Approval to David and executes only inside a reversible policy-cell with a snapshot. This planning task exercises none of the prohibited paths.

store_001_alignment.single_durable_writer: PostgreSQL+RuVector owns every durable financial macro-state class exactly once
store_001_alignment.transient_tier: redb holds live poll feeds, the statement dedup cache, and recommendation scratch; forward-only flush via envctl
store_001_alignment.ingestion_path: financial records reuse the nu_plugin CodeDB pattern: parse -> redb buffer -> envctl embed/commit -> PostgreSQL
store_001_alignment.no_sidecar: categorization vectors live in PostgreSQL via the RuVector native extension; no separate vector store
store_001_alignment.provenance_preserved: SHAKE256 witness chains and source-row lineage bind every derived financial figure to canonical PostgreSQL rows

work_items.decision: Read-only aggregation of all account balances and recent transactions into one canonical view; rung-1 only; no action.
work_items.data_authority: PostgreSQL+RuVector canonical (account_registry_and_balances, transaction_ledger_history); redb buffers the live refresh only
work_items.observation: Reads canonical balances and settled transactions; the single financial-position view that satisfies NS-CLARITY (see the whole)
work_items.simulation: sim-cell dry-run under COW branch verifies the aggregation reads only and produces no write outside the cell before the view is rendered
work_items.approval: no approval needed to observe; any future refresh that mutates external state would raise Needs-Human-Approval
work_items.reversible_cell: policy-cell, read-only FS, network denied by default; no mutation without snapshot
work_items.audit: SHAKE256 witness chain binds the rendered figures to canonical PostgreSQL rows (anti-bluffing)
work_items.rollback: restore archived financial plan; no financial account/transaction/credential touched
work_items.value_proof: David sees his whole cash position in one place without switching systems (NS-CLARITY); proven by a proof record showing the view derives solely from canonical rows
work_items.observation_precedes_action: rung 1; nothing downstream may act until this aggregation is proven

work_items.decision: Read-only semantic categorization of transactions into spend-by-category using RuVector; rung-1 observation plus derived analytics.
work_items.data_authority: PostgreSQL+RuVector canonical (transaction_categorization_embeddings computed by all-MiniLM-L6-v2 during envctl bridge, stored in PostgreSQL); redb only as f32 distance cache
work_items.observation: Reads canonical transactions and their embeddings; groups spend by category
work_items.simulation: sim-cell recomputes categories over a COW branch; MinCut/causal-graph rejects any category with no source-vector root
work_items.recommendation: none required; produces a factual breakdown (optional rung-2 note 'LifeOS suggests: review category X')
work_items.reversible_cell: policy-cell; derived rows are recomputable and disposable
work_items.audit: SHAKE256 witness chain roots each category assignment in the underlying transaction vector
work_items.rollback: re-derive from PostgreSQL ledger; no external state touched
work_items.value_proof: David understands where money goes by category; proven by reproducible re-embedding matching the stored categorization
work_items.observation_precedes_action: rung 1-2; understanding precedes any waste recommendation

work_items.decision: Read-only surfacing of upcoming bills with a bounded recommendation of pay order; payment itself is spend and stays gated.
work_items.observation: Reads canonical bill schedule and available balances; flags obligations due within a horizon
work_items.simulation: sim-cell dry-runs the pay-order recommendation against a cloned world to confirm it never emits an external payment call
work_items.recommendation: rung 2: 'LifeOS suggests:' a pay order to avoid late fees; advisory constraint only, no payment executed
work_items.approval: any actual bill payment is spend -> automatic Needs-Human-Approval to David; not exercised in this planning slice
work_items.reversible_cell: policy-cell; a future approved payment would run only with snapshot + rollback point in a dev-cell/policy-cell
work_items.audit: SHAKE256 witness chain records the recommendation and its ledger basis
work_items.rollback: restore archived financial plan; no payment, account, or credential touched
work_items.value_proof: avoided late fees and missed obligations (self-sustaining value); proven by the recommendation deriving from canonical due dates with no external call in the sim-cell trace
work_items.observation_precedes_action: rung 1-2 only; the spend action is explicitly deferred behind human approval
work_items.spine_path: memory(PostgreSQL) -> simulation(sim-cell) -> recommendation -> [approval gate STOP] -> proof

work_items.decision: Read-only detection of recurring charges with a bounded cancellation recommendation; cancellation is external action and stays gated.
work_items.observation: Reads canonical transaction history; detects recurring/duplicate charges and idle subscriptions
work_items.simulation: sim-cell recomputes detections over a COW branch; verifies no cancel request is emitted
work_items.recommendation: rung 2: 'LifeOS suggests:' cancel/renegotiate specific subscriptions; advisory only
work_items.approval: any cancellation is external financial action -> Needs-Human-Approval; not exercised here
work_items.reversible_cell: policy-cell; a future approved cancellation runs only with snapshot + rollback
work_items.audit: SHAKE256 witness chain roots each detection in the underlying recurring transactions
work_items.rollback: re-derive from PostgreSQL; no subscription or account touched
work_items.value_proof: recovered recurring waste directly funds self-sustained growth (MVP1 domain); proven by detections tracing to real recurring ledger rows
work_items.observation_precedes_action: rung 1-2; the cancellation action is deferred behind human approval
work_items.spine_path: memory(PostgreSQL derived) -> simulation(sim-cell) -> recommendation -> [approval gate STOP]

work_items.surface: Budget vs actual variance and alert
work_items.decision: Read-only comparison of actual spend against David-defined budgets with a bounded overrun alert; no enforcement action.
work_items.observation: Reads canonical budgets and categorized actuals; computes per-category variance
work_items.simulation: sim-cell computes variance over a COW branch and confirms alerts are advisory-only
work_items.recommendation: rung 2: 'LifeOS suggests:' attention on overrun categories; advisory constraint
work_items.approval: no external action; any future spend control would raise Needs-Human-Approval
work_items.reversible_cell: policy-cell; variance rows recomputable and disposable
work_items.audit: SHAKE256 witness chain binds each variance figure to budget rows and categorized transactions
work_items.rollback: re-derive from PostgreSQL; no external state touched
work_items.value_proof: David directs spend as one system (NS-DIRECTION), catching overruns early; proven by variance reproducing from canonical budget + actuals
work_items.observation_precedes_action: rung 1-2; no enforcement without human approval

work_items.decision: Read-only aggregation plus ESN simulation of Element Ark runway/burn; forecast only, never a real-world action.
work_items.data_authority: PostgreSQL+RuVector canonical (account_registry_and_balances, transaction_ledger_history as initial conditions); runway_forecast_simulation_state non-authoritative in the sim-cell
work_items.observation: Reads canonical balances and historical burn as initial conditions
work_items.simulation: ATAS/ESN ensemble forecasts runway up to N steps ahead (blueprint resource-burn maps to financial burn); failed worlds become failure capsules, never baseline
work_items.recommendation: rung 2: 'LifeOS suggests:' runway-extending focus areas; advisory only
work_items.approval: no external action; any capital move is spend/trading -> Needs-Human-Approval
work_items.reversible_cell: sim-cell; only a proven forecast summary may persist to PostgreSQL
work_items.audit: SHAKE256 witness chain roots the forecast in the canonical initial-condition rows
work_items.rollback: discard the simulated worlds; restore archived plan; nothing external touched
work_items.value_proof: David sees self-sustaining runway for Element Ark (MVP1 self-sustained growth); proven by the forecast deriving solely from canonical initial conditions with failed worlds quarantined
work_items.observation_precedes_action: rung 1 (aggregate) then simulate; no action rung reachable without human approval
work_items.spine_path: memory(PostgreSQL initial conditions) -> simulation(ATAS/ESN sim-cell) -> proof

work_items.decision: Read-only matching of incoming payments to invoices with a bounded follow-up recommendation; no external collection action.
work_items.data_authority: PostgreSQL+RuVector canonical (transaction_ledger_history, obligation_and_bill_schedule for receivable side)
work_items.observation: Reads canonical incoming transactions and outstanding invoices; matches paid vs unpaid
work_items.simulation: sim-cell dry-runs the match over a COW branch; confirms no dunning/collection message is emitted
work_items.recommendation: rung 2: 'LifeOS suggests:' follow up on specific unpaid invoices; advisory only
work_items.approval: any external follow-up/collection is external action -> Needs-Human-Approval; not exercised here
work_items.reversible_cell: policy-cell; match rows recomputable and disposable
work_items.audit: SHAKE256 witness chain roots each match in the paired invoice and payment rows
work_items.rollback: re-derive from PostgreSQL; no message, account, or credential touched
work_items.value_proof: faster collection of receivables sustains company cash flow (MVP1 self-sustained growth); proven by matches tracing to real invoice+payment rows
work_items.observation_precedes_action: rung 1-2; the follow-up action is deferred behind human approval

work_items.decision: Read-only aggregation of stock/crypto positions with live quotes; trading is explicitly prohibited and never defined as executable.
work_items.data_authority: PostgreSQL+RuVector canonical (portfolio_positions); live_balance_and_quote_poll_feed transient on redb, flushed forward
work_items.observation: Reads canonical positions; overlays transient live quotes buffered in redb
work_items.simulation: sim-cell verifies the aggregation is read-only and emits no order to any broker
work_items.recommendation: none that implies trading; at most an advisory 'LifeOS suggests: review concentration', never an order
work_items.approval: trading is an automatic prohibited path -> would require explicit David authority; not exercised and not defined as executable in this slice
work_items.reversible_cell: policy-cell; positions read-only; quotes transient and disposable
work_items.audit: SHAKE256 witness chain roots reported positions in canonical rows; any order attempt has no causal edge and is rejected
work_items.rollback: drop transient quotes; restore archived plan; no brokerage account or order touched
work_items.value_proof: David sees total portfolio position across accounts (NS-CLARITY) with zero trading capability; proven by the sim-cell trace showing no broker order path exists
work_items.observation_precedes_action: rung 1 only; trading is structurally excluded, not merely deferred
work_items.spine_path: memory(PostgreSQL canonical + redb transient quotes) -> simulation(sim-cell, no-order proof) -> proof

execution_order_rationale: Aggregation (W1) and categorization (W2) establish the read-only base; variance (W5) needs categories; bill/subscription/receivables recommendations (W3/W4/W7) build on the base; runway forecast (W6) needs history; portfolio (W8) is independent read-only. Every earlier rung is proven before a later workflow relies on it.
completion_gate: Each of the 8 financial workflows carries a single canonical data authority (PostgreSQL+RuVector per STORE-001), a simulation stage, a mandatory human-approval gate for any state-changing rung, explicit spend/trading/account/credential/external prohibitions, a reversible policy-cell, a SHAKE256 audit trail, a rollback that touches no real financial account, and a self-sustaining value proof; observation precedes action in every workflow; all 13 financial state classes are assigned exactly once; and MVP1-001.proof.json records product_execution_performed=false with no prohibited path exercised.
